title: Petrology & Structural Geology
outline: This course covers key concepts in igneous, metamorphic and sedimentary petrology in combination with structural geology. Topics include: interpreting major and trace element and isotope variations in igneous rocks; origin and evolution of the major magma series; thermodynamics, kinetics and chemography of metamorphic reactions; tectonic setting of metamorphic terrains; principles of interpretation and classification of continental and marine sedimentary environments; kinematic principles, deformation mechanisms, microstructure, faulting and tectonic geomorphology.
dp_requirements: Attendance at 80% of practicals and an average of 30% in all marked classwork and tests.
assessment: Classwork counts 20%; one 4-hour practical examination written in June counts 30%; one 3-hour theory examination written in June counts 50%. Sub-minima of 40% required in both practical and theory examination papers.
